note_data.title: Partial Fractions
keyPoints: Partial fractions is a method of expressing a rational fraction as a sum of simpler fractions
The degree of numerator must be less than the degree of denominator for partial fraction decomposition
If degree of numerator ≥ degree of denominator, first perform polynomial long division
For linear factors (ax + b), partial fraction has form A/(ax + b)
For repeated linear factors (ax + b)², use A/(ax + b) + B/(ax + b)²
For quadratic factors (ax² + bx + c), partial fraction has form (Ax + B)/(ax² + bx + c)
Always find LCM to combine fractions and equate coefficients to solve for constants
Method of substitution can be used when suitable values make calculation easier

definitions.definition: A method of expressing a proper rational fraction as a sum of two or more simpler fractions whose denominators are factors of the original denominator
A rational fraction in which the degree of the numerator is less than the degree of the denominator
A rational fraction in which the degree of the numerator is greater than or equal to the degree of the denominator

importantQuestions.question: Define partial fractions and write the partial fraction form for (3x + 1)/((x + 1)(x - 2))
importantQuestions.answer: Partial fractions is a method of expressing a proper rational fraction as a sum of simpler fractions. The form is: (3x + 1)/((x + 1)(x - 2)) = A/(x + 1) + B/(x - 2)

commonMistakes: Students forget to check if the fraction is proper before decomposing
Writing wrong partial fraction form for repeated factors
Making calculation errors when substituting values to find constants
Not finding common denominator correctly when combining fractions
Forgetting to factorize denominator completely before starting decomposition
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 long question worth 5-8 marks in BSEK papers
Always write the general form first before solving for constants
Use substitution method for simple linear factors - it's faster
Practice factoring quadratic expressions as this is essential for partial fractions
Show all steps clearly - BSEK gives marks for method even if final answer is wrong
formulas: For (px + q)/((ax + b)(cx + d)) = A/(ax + b) + B/(cx + d)
For (px + q)/(ax + b)² = A/(ax + b) + B/(ax + b)²
For (px² + qx + r)/((ax + b)(cx² + dx + e)) = A/(ax + b) + (Bx + C)/(cx² + dx + e)
note_data.keyDiagram: No specific diagram required for this chapter, but students should clearly show the step-by-step algebraic working when equating coefficients and solving for partial fraction constants A, B, C etc.
